question: In July 2, 1959 United States standards for grades of processed fruits, vegetables, and certain other products listed as dehydrated, consider the items in the "dried and dehydrated section" specifically marked as dehydrated along with any items in the Frozen/Chilled section that contain the whole name of the item, but not if they're marked Chilled. As of August 2023, what is the percentage (to the nearest percent) of those standards that have been superseded by a new version since the date given in the 1959 standards?
answer: Unable to determine due to data access failures

tool_calls.name: sandbox_exec

arguments.code:
# Extract the standards from the 1959 document that match the criteria

# Items in the "dried and dehydrated section" specifically marked as dehydrated
dehydrated_items = [
    ("Apples, Dehydrated (Low-moisture)", "November 29, 1955"),
    ("Grapefruit Juice (Dehydrated)", "March 19, 1956"), 
    ("Orange Juice (Dehydrated)", "January 27, 1956")
]

# Items in Frozen/Chilled section that contain the whole name but NOT marked as Chilled
frozen_chilled_items = [
    ("Apples", "May 17, 1954"),
    ("Apricots", "June 30, 1945"),
    ("Asparagus", "April 18, 1952"),
    ("Beans, Green and Wax", "August 25, 1954"),
    ("Beans, Lima", "April 16, 1957"),
    ("Berries, (Blackberries, Boysenberries, etc.)", "August 15, 1946"),
    ("Blueberries", "May 7, 1955"),
    ("Broccoli", "March 1, 1959"),
    ("Brussels Sprouts", "May 11, 1951"),
    ("Carrots, Diced", "March 30, 1953"),
    ("Cauliflower", "November 12, 1951"),
    ("Cherries, Red Sour (Tart) Pitted", "June 18, 1949"),
    ("Cherries, Sweet", "March 1, 1958"),
    ("Corn-on-the-Cob", "May 15, 1958"),
    ("Corn, Whole Kernel (or Whole Grain)", "August 1, 1952"),
    ("Grapefruit", "February 20, 1948"),
    ("Grapefruit Juice, Concentrated", "December 28, 1956"),
    ("Grapefruit Juice and Orange Juice, Concentrated, Blended", "December 10, 1951"),
    ("Grape Juice, Concentrated Sweetened", "November 1, 1957"),
    ("Leafy Greens", "July 1, 1957"),  # Based on text about revised regulations
    ("Lemonade, Frozen Concentrate", "February 1, 1958"),
    ("Limeade, Frozen Concentrate", "November 2, 1956"),
    ("Okra", "March 16, 1959"),
    ("Orange Juice, Concentrated", "December 1, 1955"),
    ("Peaches", "June 30, 1945"),
    ("Peas", "May 28, 1959"),
    ("Peas and Carrots", "March 20, 1955"),
    ("Peas, Field and Black-eye", "October 15, 1951"),
    ("Peppers, Sweet", "March 13, 1959"),
    ("Pineapple", "January 25, 1949"),
    ("Plums", "March 6, 1956"),
    ("Potatoes, French Fried", "November 20, 1954"),
    ("Raspberries", "August 16, 1948"),
    ("Rhubarb", "August 15, 1945"),
    ("Spinach", "October 26, 1951"),
    ("Squash (Cooked)", "October 5, 1953"),
    ("Squash (Summer Type)", "April 3, 1953"),
    ("Strawberries", "February 1, 1958"),
    ("Succotash", "March 6, 1959"),
    ("Turnip Greens with Turnips", "August 19, 1958"),
    ("Vegetables, Mixed", "May 24, 1954")
]

# Note: Excluding "Orange Juice, Chilled" because it's specifically marked as Chilled

# Combine all items
all_items = dehydrated_items + frozen_chilled_items

print(f"Dehydrated items: {len(dehydrated_items)}")
print(f"Frozen/Chilled items (excluding 'Chilled'): {len(frozen_chilled_items)}")
print(f"Total items to check: {len(all_items)}")
print()
print("All items:")
for i, (item, date) in enumerate(all_items, 1):
    print(f"{i:2d}. {item} - {date}")

Annotator Metadata.Steps:
1. Searched "July 2, 1959 United States standards for grades of processed fruits, vegetables, and certain other products" on Google.
2. Opened https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/0/06/United_States_standards_for_grades_of_processed_fruits%2C_vegetables%2C_and_certain_other_products_%28as_of_July_2%2C_1959%29_%28IA_unitedstatesstan14unit_4%29.pdf.
3. Scrolled to the "DRIED or DEHYDRATED" section.
4. Opened a new tab and searched "united states standards for grades of dehydrated apples".
5. Opened https://www.ams.usda.gov/grades-standards/dehydrated-apples-grades-and-standards.
6. Opened the "U.S. Grade Standards for Dehydrated Apples (pdf)" PDF.
7. Checked the date against the 1959 standards.
8. Repeated steps 4-7 for all dehydrated items in the "DRIED or DEHYDRATED" section:
9. Grapefruit Juice, updated (running tally: 2/2)
10. Orange Juice, updated (running tally: 3/3)
11. Found all versions of the dehydrated items in Frozen or Chilled, except those marked Chilled: Apples; Grapefruit Juice, Concentrated; Grapefruit Juice and Orange Juice, Concentrated, Blended; Orange Juice, Concentrated
12. Repeated steps 4-7 all those versions:
13. Apples, not updated (running tally: 3/4)
14. Grapefruit Juice, Concentrated, updated (running tally: 4/5)
15. Grapefruit Juice and Orange Juice, Concentrated, Blended, updated (running tally: 5/6)
16. Orange Juice, Concentrated, updated (running tally: 6/7)
17. Calculated the percentage (6 / 7 * 100% = 85.7%).
18. Rounded to the nearest percent (86%).
Annotator Metadata.Tools:
1. Web browser
2. Search engine
3. PDF access
4. Calculator
